tests_rerun.command: python -c "import pathlib, yaml; [yaml.safe_load(p.read_text(encoding='utf-8')) for p in pathlib.Path('.github/workflows').glob('phase5-*.yml')]; print('workflow syntax: PASS')"

blocking_findings.issue: The secret tree scan self-triggers on the scaffold tree because the secret-pattern regex literals are themselves inside the scanned source tree.
blocking_findings.evidence: python phase5/scripts/lint_phase5_secrets.py --root phase5 -> flags the guard module itself

blocking_findings.issue: The implementation report contains literal secret and forbidden-analysis fixture strings, so the root lints fail on committed evidence artifacts.
blocking_findings.evidence: python phase5/scripts/lint_phase5_secrets.py --root phase5 and python phase5/scripts/lint_phase5_forbidden_analysis.py --root phase5

blocking_findings.issue: The source CI scaffold is materially smaller than the parent plan requires and omits integration, schema, golden-vector, state-machine, and checkpoint/resume coverage.
blocking_findings.evidence: Parent plan lines 409-420 and 244-245 require those check classes.

blocking_findings.issue: Evidence integrity is scoped to the implementation report instead of compact checkpoint evidence, so it does not yet match the parent plan.
blocking_findings.evidence: Parent plan lines 428-430 describe committed checkpoint-manifest validation.

frozen_artifact_integrity.details: No phase4/ or phase4_5/ files changed in the audited commit; frozen-input hashes were verified against the parent plans and registry.

secret_scope_result.details: The root secret scan fails on the scaffold tree as committed, so the secret guard is not yet passable in CI.
